meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Scaling Student SaaS Projects
content.seo_description: Stop hitting walls in your student projects. Master debugging with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your SaaS builds from prototype to production.
content.answer_block: To scale student projects, you must shift from manual troubleshooting to AI-assisted architectural analysis. By integrating Antigravity for dependency visualization and Claude Code for iterative refactoring, you can identify bottlenecks in minutes, automate complex debugging workflows, and transform brittle student codebases into production-ready, scalable software architecture.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen filled with red error logs and spaghetti code, wondering why your project refuses to scale. You have the logic, but the sheer complexity of your growing repository has become a labyrinth that feels impossible to navigate. This is the 'Intermediate Wall'—the exact moment where most student developers give up.

But the rules of software development have shifted. You no longer need to be a solitary hero debugging every semicolon by hand. By leveraging AI agents, you can treat your codebase as a data structure, allowing intelligent tools to map your dependencies and resolve architectural debt in real-time. It is time to stop struggling and start engineering.

steps.title: Mapping Architectural Bottlenecks
steps.description: Before fixing bugs, visualize how your services interact to identify where the scaling friction occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project dependency tree and identify all circular dependencies or high-latency bottlenecks in the API service layer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map project architecture and output dependency graph'

steps.title: Automated Bug Isolation
steps.description: Use AI to create a minimal reproduction script for intermittent errors that appear only under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that simulates concurrent user requests to isolate the memory leak occurring in the auth module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate stress test for auth module and log failures'

steps.title: Refactoring for Modularity
steps.description: Break down monolith files into clean, decoupled components using AI-driven refactoring patterns.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the main controller into smaller, single-responsibility modules following SOLID principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'refactor controller into modular services'

steps.title: Implementing Robust Error Handling
steps.description: Transition from basic console logs to a centralized, AI-monitored error reporting strategy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a global error handler that categorizes exceptions and suggests potential root causes based on stack traces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add global error middleware with diagnostics'

steps.title: Performance Optimization & Scaling
steps.description: Apply AI insights to optimize database queries and implement caching strategies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Identify slow database queries and suggest indexing strategies or caching layers to improve throughput by 40%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'optimize database queries and suggest caching'

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your 'context window' by including the relevant file tree before asking for deep architectural changes.
Use AI to generate documentation for your code as you write it; this forces you to explain the logic, which often reveals the bug itself.
Treat AI-generated code as a 'draft'—always perform a manual code review to ensure the logic aligns with your project's specific constraints.
Create a custom 'system prompt' file in your root directory to guide Claude Code on your preferred coding style and project architecture.
When debugging, ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the fix to turn every error into a learning milestone for your portfolio.
content.next_steps: Once your code is stable, deploy your project to a cloud provider like Vercel or Railway and document the performance metrics in your portfolio to showcase your engineering maturity.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to turn your complex project into a scalable, production-grade application. Don't let technical debt hold your potential back. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
